planCourses.Description: In English Composition I students learn reflective, analytical and argumentative writing strategies, incorporating sources and personal experience. Students will negotiate between public and private rhetorical situations and purposes to achieve academic literacy. They will write multiple drafts using a recursive writing process as they work toward fluency in style and mechanics. (PreReq: DEV-0032 OR DEV-0044)
planCourses.courseDescription: In English Composition I students learn reflective, analytical and argumentative writing strategies, incorporating sources and personal experience. Students will negotiate between public and private rhetorical situations and purposes to achieve academic literacy. They will write multiple drafts using a recursive writing process as they work toward fluency in style and mechanics. (PreReq: DEV-0032 OR DEV-0044)

planCourses.Description: Focusing on development of effective small group decision-making and leadership skills, stressing better methods of expressing oneself and understanding others through learning group communication, theory and participating in small group decision-making experiences. (PreReq: DEV-0012 AND DEV-0032 OR DEV-0044)
planCourses.courseDescription: Focusing on development of effective small group decision-making and leadership skills, stressing better methods of expressing oneself and understanding others through learning group communication, theory and participating in small group decision-making experiences. (PreReq: DEV-0012 AND DEV-0032 OR DEV-0044)
planCourses.studentNotes: Small group communication is recommended due to the practical experiences and activities in leadership and group decision -making. COM 2211 is also allowed.

planCourses.Description: Development of accounting standards, conceptual framework of financial accounting. Review of the accounting cycle, preparation of income statement, statement of owner's equity, balance sheet, statement of cash flows and additional reporting issues. Time value of money, current asset accounting and reporting. (PreReq: ACC-1220 AND ACC-1510)
planCourses.courseDescription: Development of accounting standards, conceptual framework of financial accounting. Review of the accounting cycle, preparation of income statement, statement of owner's equity, balance sheet, statement of cash flows and additional reporting issues. Time value of money, current asset accounting and reporting. (PreReq: ACC-1220 AND ACC-1510)

planCourses.PreReqs: ACC 2101 and Other (with a grade of C or better)
planCourses.Description: Transaction analysis and financial reporting for long-term assets, long-term liabilities and stockholder's equity. (PreReq: ACC-2101)
planCourses.courseDescription: Transaction analysis and financial reporting for long-term assets, long-term liabilities and stockholder's equity. (PreReq: ACC-2101)

planCourses.Description: Basic economic principles with macro sequence. Interrelationship of households, business and government with an examination of Keynesian theory, fiscal policy and monetary policy. (PreReq: DEV-0012 AND DEV-0020)
planCourses.courseDescription: Basic economic principles with macro sequence. Interrelationship of households, business and government with an examination of Keynesian theory, fiscal policy and monetary policy. (PreReq: DEV-0012 AND DEV-0020)

planCourses.Description: This course uses accounting and finance concepts to effectively analyze and manage business finances. (PreReq: ACC-1220)
planCourses.courseDescription: This course uses accounting and finance concepts to effectively analyze and manage business finances. (PreReq: ACC-1220)

planCourses.Description: Application of cost accounting concepts and techniques to complex problems in manufacturing accounting and service firms. (PreReq: ACC-1220 AND ACC-1510)
planCourses.courseDescription: Application of cost accounting concepts and techniques to complex problems in manufacturing accounting and service firms. (PreReq: ACC-1220 AND ACC-1510)

planCourses.PreReqs: MAT 0300 and Other (with a grade of C or better or satisfactory score on math placement test)
planCourses.Description: Statistical techniques and methodology. Graphical and tabular presentation of data, probability, parameters, statistical distributions, sampling, confidence intervals and tests of hypotheses. Three classroom, two lab hours per week. (PreReq: MAT-1460 OR MAT-1470)
planCourses.courseDescription: Statistical techniques and methodology. Graphical and tabular presentation of data, probability, parameters, statistical distributions, sampling, confidence intervals and tests of hypotheses. Three classroom, two lab hours per week. (PreReq: MAT-1460 OR MAT-1470)

planCourses.PreReqs: MAT 0300 and Other (with a grade of C or better or satisfactory score on math placement test)
planCourses.Description: Applications of finite mathematics and functions to business analysis. Functions, financial mathematics, systems, matrices, inequalities, linear programming, sets, permutations and combinations and elementary probability and statistics. (PreReq: MAT-1365 OR MAT-1370)
planCourses.courseDescription: Applications of finite mathematics and functions to business analysis. Functions, financial mathematics, systems, matrices, inequalities, linear programming, sets, permutations and combinations and elementary probability and statistics. (PreReq: MAT-1365 OR MAT-1370)
planCourses.studentNotes: Finite Math is recommended due to the application of skills to business analysis.  Alternative course is Mat 1470 - College Algebra.

planCourses.Description: An introduction to preparation and use of accounting reports for business entities; focus on uses of accounting for external reporting, emphasizing accounting as a provider of financial information. (PreReq: DEV-0012 AND DEV-0020)
planCourses.courseDescription: An introduction to preparation and use of accounting reports for business entities; focus on uses of accounting for external reporting, emphasizing accounting as a provider of financial information. (PreReq: DEV-0012 AND DEV-0020)

planCourses.Description: An introduction to the use of accounting information by managers. Topics include the use of accounting information for planning and control, performance evaluation, decision-making and the statement of cash flows, along with financial statement analysis. (PreReq: ACC-1210)
planCourses.courseDescription: An introduction to the use of accounting information by managers. Topics include the use of accounting information for planning and control, performance evaluation, decision-making and the statement of cash flows, along with financial statement analysis. (PreReq: ACC-1210)

planCourses.Description: Introductory course including the basic tax model, personal gross income, personal deductions and credits, property transactions, special tax computations and introduction to corporate taxation.
planCourses.courseDescription: Introductory course including the basic tax model, personal gross income, personal deductions and credits, property transactions, special tax computations and introduction to corporate taxation.

planCourses.Description: Review of accounting information systems and an overview of auditing. Covers internal controls and system documentation, transaction processing and databases, professional standard and ethics. Review of legal liability, audit evidence, risk evaluation and audit planning, audit procedures and audit reports. (PreReq: ACC-2101)
planCourses.courseDescription: Review of accounting information systems and an overview of auditing. Covers internal controls and system documentation, transaction processing and databases, professional standard and ethics. Review of legal liability, audit evidence, risk evaluation and audit planning, audit procedures and audit reports. (PreReq: ACC-2101)

planCourses.Description: Major trends in the development of Western culture, emphasizing political, economic, social and cultural achievements, from prehistory to the seventeenth century.
planCourses.courseDescription: Major trends in the development of Western culture, emphasizing political, economic, social and cultural achievements, from prehistory to the seventeenth century.
planCourses.studentNotes: HIS 1111 is recommended for the Arts and Humanities elective because it provides an understanding of the early development of modern society.  A student may choose from any other Ohio Transfer Module Arts and Humanities http://www.sinclair.edu/transfer/gened/module.
planCourses.contactNotes: Taking the Arts and Humanities electives in the Summer will enable  students take 5 courses and 15 credit hours in their Second Year.

planCourses.Description: The American legal system as it relates to business transactions, including the judicial system and sources of law, legal procedures, torts, business ethics and social responsibility, contracts, property, employment law, agency, partnerships and corporations.
planCourses.courseDescription: The American legal system as it relates to business transactions, including the judicial system and sources of law, legal procedures, torts, business ethics and social responsibility, contracts, property, employment law, agency, partnerships and corporations.

planCourses.Description: The American business system and basic principles of the free market system. Includes introduction of business concepts, entrepreneurship, management, marketing, economics, accounting and other important business principles.
planCourses.courseDescription: The American business system and basic principles of the free market system. Includes introduction of business concepts, entrepreneurship, management, marketing, economics, accounting and other important business principles.

planCourses.Description: Microeconomic theory including price theory, the theory of the firm, resource demand and wage determination. Also includes public policy toward business, economic inequality, labor, trade, balance of payments and the economics of third-world nations. (PreReq: DEV-0012 AND DEV-0020)
planCourses.courseDescription: Microeconomic theory including price theory, the theory of the firm, resource demand and wage determination. Also includes public policy toward business, economic inequality, labor, trade, balance of payments and the economics of third-world nations. (PreReq: DEV-0012 AND DEV-0020)

planCourses.Description: Use word processing, spreadsheet, database and presentation software applications to create reports, spreadsheets, databases and presentations for business and other applications.
planCourses.courseDescription: Use word processing, spreadsheet, database and presentation software applications to create reports, spreadsheets, databases and presentations for business and other applications.
planCourses.contactNotes: If student is proficient on the computer. Program chairperson would consider substituting for this requirement please contact for further information.

planCourses.Description: This course uses general ledger software and spreadsheets to record, report and analyze accounting information. (PreReq: ACC-1210 AND BIS-1410)
planCourses.courseDescription: This course uses general ledger software and spreadsheets to record, report and analyze accounting information. (PreReq: ACC-1210 AND BIS-1410)

planCourses.Description: Marketing strategies and decision making in the context of other business functions. Topics include: research and analysis of markets, environments and competition; market segmentation and selection of target markets; consumer and organizational behavior; planning and integration of product, price, promotion and distribution activities for profit and nonprofit, domestic and global settings. (PreReq: ECO-2180)
planCourses.courseDescription: Marketing strategies and decision making in the context of other business functions. Topics include: research and analysis of markets, environments and competition; market segmentation and selection of target markets; consumer and organizational behavior; planning and integration of product, price, promotion and distribution activities for profit and nonprofit, domestic and global settings. (PreReq: ECO-2180)

planCourses.Description: Course covers tax research, federal, state and local business income tax returns and preparation of information returns, tax filings for trusts, estates and nonprofit organizations, and the reporting of advanced tax compliance issues for individual and business tax returns. (PreReq: ACC-2321)
planCourses.courseDescription: Course covers tax research, federal, state and local business income tax returns and preparation of information returns, tax filings for trusts, estates and nonprofit organizations, and the reporting of advanced tax compliance issues for individual and business tax returns. (PreReq: ACC-2321)
planCourses.studentNotes:
ACC 2322 - Advanced Taxation is recommended to earn the Tax Practitioner Certificate and for preparation to take the IRS Enrolled Agent Certificate exam. There are alternative courses which satisfy the business elective
Other options:  ACC-2510, ACC 2700, MAN-2110, MAN-1110, MAN-1106, CIS-1111, MRK-2145, OR LAW-1102
planCourses.contactNotes: There are alternative courses from which students may choose. The Advanced Accounting course requires a third Spring semester due to the series of pre-requisite course.

mapData.name: ACC.S.AAS_2016_FT
mapData.studentNotes:
Pre-Program Considerations:	COMPLETE ANY REQUIRED DEVELOPMENTAL (DEV) COURSES								
This Degree is not designed to meet traditional bachelor requirements. Students who complete the accounting program can qualify to sit for CPA exam in Ohio but they will need to meet additional qualification set by the governing association.		
THE CPA Test is administered by the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants (AICPA). The Accountancy Board of Ohio is the governing body that sets the requirements for Ohio.
mapData.contactNotes: Graduates can anticipate to earn $10 - $12 with little to no experience.  It is possible to make more based on experience and additional qualifications.
